title: Quantitative thresholds over boolean checks
description: Replaced boolean deletion detection with quantitative threshold (>50 lines AND >70% ratio) in git-check v2. Eliminated false positives from minor edits while catching significant data loss.
context: Original git-check flagged ANY file with deletions, causing alert fatigue. User reported glitches from imprecise detection.
outcome: More accurate detection with measurable criteria. Users can reason about why something was flagged.

title: Content-aware pattern scanning with context detection
description: Implemented markdown backtick counting to skip pattern matches inside inline code spans. Scans content for sensitive data patterns but intelligently skips documentation examples.
context: Sensitive data scanner triggering on security pattern examples in README files. Need to detect real credentials without flagging documentation.
outcome: Eliminated false positives in documentation while maintaining real credential detection. Scanner now context-aware.

title: Meaningful exit codes for blocking behavior
description: Implemented semantic exit codes (0=clean, 1=block, 2=git error, 3=invalid state) to enable git-check to actually block commits in automation. Previously always returned 0 regardless of issues found.
context: Git-check couldn't integrate with pre-commit hooks or CI/CD because it had no way to signal failure programmatically.
outcome: Can now be used in git hooks with `exit $?` pattern. Automation can distinguish success from various failure modes.

title: Protected file patterns for architectural safety
description: Created comprehensive protected file pattern list covering core configs (N5/config/*.json), schemas, architectural knowledge, and lists registry. Flags any modifications to critical system files.
context: Need to prevent accidental modification of system-critical files that would break N5 infrastructure.
outcome: 18 protected patterns covering configuration, schemas, architectural docs, and registries. Prevents catastrophic changes.

title: Ask clarifying questions before implementation
description: Identified 6 ambiguities (deletion threshold, file size limit, protected files, blocking behavior, scanning depth, glitch history) and asked targeted questions before building. Prevented rework.
context: User rule requires asking 3+ clarifying questions when in doubt. Complex refactoring with many design decisions.
outcome: Single implementation cycle with no major rework. All design decisions aligned with user needs from the start.
